disambiguation_rules.the commission: Federal Trade Commission — resolved from context of FTC Act enforcement provisions
disambiguation_rules.the secretary: Secretary of Commerce — inferred from Title VIII context on covered national security transactions and commercial technology control
disambiguation_rules.appropriate congressional committees: Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and Committee on Financial Services — explicitly named in section 803(d)
disambiguation_rules.the assistant secretary: Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Analysis — resolved from earlier extractions and Title V context on supply chain responsibilities
disambiguation_rules.the working group: Supply Chain Resilience Working Group — explicitly named in section 503(a) as the statutory designation

oddities.note: Document provided is only a title and summary without substantive legislative text. The extraction is based on the summary description rather than detailed statutory language, which may result in incomplete entity identification.

Document text provided is the substantive legislative language of Title VIII, establishing detailed investment prohibition and notification regimes with specific procedural requirements. The Secretary referenced throughout is contextually the Secretary of the Treasury based on investment transaction authority, though not explicitly named in the provided excerpt.

Document provided is title and summary only without substantive legislative text. Title V heading indicates content scope but detailed statutory language is absent. Extraction based on summary description rather than explicit statutory provisions.

llm_disambiguation_log.resolved: Federal Trade Commission — resolved from context of FTC Act enforcement provisions
Secretary of Commerce — inferred from Title VIII context on covered national security transactions and commercial technology control
Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and Committee on Financial Services — explicitly named in section 803(d)
Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Analysis — resolved from earlier extractions and Title V context on supply chain responsibilities
Supply Chain Resilience Working Group — explicitly named in section 503(a) as the statutory designation
Secretary of Commerce — inferred from subsection (h) context regarding private entity interactions with Commerce Department officials
